mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in retail sales signals accelerating household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, so higher 3-month growth in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 vintage retail sales level was 442032 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if walk-forward tests show RSAFS 3-month growth has Rank IC <= 0 over a 3-year rolling window or if top-quintile RSAFS momentum does not beat bottom-quintile momentum in 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate liftoff from the zero lower bound raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, so an upward crossing in FEDFUNDS predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% after being at or below 0.25% for at least 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 vintage federal funds rate was 0.13 percent
failure_condition: Reject if triggered liftoff episodes produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances or if average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals weakening income growth and recession risk, so a sharp increase in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 vintage unemployment rate was 5.3 percent
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of observations or if mean triggered returns are not statistically below baseline at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices accelerate while the policy rate remains pinned, markets price future tightening before earnings improve, so rising CPIAUCSL combined with low FEDFUNDS predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth rises for 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:May 2015 vintage CPIAUCSL was 237.031 and June 2015 FEDFUNDS was 0.13 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPI acceleration and low-FEDFUNDS trigger has nonnegative average 20-day excess returns versus all days or if the interaction term is insignificant with p > 0.10 in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals weakening goods demand and operating leverage pressure, so negative momentum in INDPRO predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 vintage industrial production index was 105.7065
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances or if triggered mean returns are not below unconditional 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in housing starts signals stronger construction activity, durable-goods demand, and household balance-sheet confidence, so HOUST strength predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses more than 5% above its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:May 2015 vintage housing starts were 1036 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upside-crossing signals have a hit rate below the unconditional positive-return rate or if their average 60-day return is not greater than baseline in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion paired with a falling unemployment rate signals broad labor-income growth without recession stress, so the joint improvement in PAYEMS and UNRATE predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by more than 450 thousand over 3 months and UNRATE falls over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 vintage PAYEMS was 141842 thousand and UNRATE was 5.3 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-improvement trigger does not outperform PAYEMS-only and UNRATE-only signals in 20-day mean returns or if its walk-forward hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When real output growth and payroll growth are both positive while unemployment is already low, the expansion is late-cycle and stronger activity raises margin and tightening risks, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth is positive, PAYEMS 3-month change is positive, UNRATE is below 5.5%, and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Latest available 2015 snapshot showed low FEDFUNDS and UNRATE near 5.3 percent
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with pending monetary normalization
failure_condition: Reject if this late-cycle interaction trigger produces positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the interaction model fails to beat an unconditional benchmark out of sample.
